Summary: Are special characters excluded in full text search inside profile pages (Activity search)?
Description:
Hey team, 


One of my clients pointed out that, from any records' profile, *they are not able to search on Notes just by typing specific characters, such as "%".* To find the relevant notes added with this content, they actually need to type "20%" or "30%", for example.

The issue they pointed out is that they usually don't remember the fee discussed with the client when discussing job details, for example, and since they're storing on Notes, they would like to find the note where they added the % easily.

Is this a known limitation, or should it be logged as a bug (reproducible on my end)?

CC: [~accountid:712020:9e4be7f2-ce08-4d42-a7e8-6c5789fc853d]
Comments:
25/Feb/26 7:34 PM;5f7aab31bd1298006fe71331;[~accountid:712020:f09934f9-c0ad-4594-84e7-19f363dfd836] 
Full-text search will not work for searching the {{%}} character due to how SingleStore processes text during indexing.

When SingleStore creates a full-text index, it breaks the text into individual words (a process called _tokenization_). During this process, special characters such as {{%}}, {{+}}, {{-}}, {{!}}, etc., are automatically removed.

So, if your data contains *"20%"*, the full-text index will store only *"20"* - the {{%}} symbol is stripped out during indexing. As a result, when you search for *"20%"*, it effectively searches for *"20"*, and you will get results for any record containing "20", regardless of whether it originally had a {{%}} after it.
---
25/Feb/26 7:55 PM;712020:f09934f9-c0ad-4594-84e7-19f363dfd836;Ohh, got it. Thanks for the clear explanation, [~accountid:5f7aab31bd1298006fe71331].

So this isn’t something we can change on our end for now, correct? Would it make sense to check with SingleStore if there are plans to support this, or are these special characters always removed by default?
---
25/Feb/26 8:12 PM;5f7aab31bd1298006fe71331;[~accountid:712020:f09934f9-c0ad-4594-84e7-19f363dfd836] 

This is not possible with *Full-Text Search* because that tool only looks for *actual words*.

Think of it like a dictionary: you can look up the word 'Apple', but you can't look up the '%' symbol. When the system 'reads' your data to create the search list, it simply skips over special characters like they aren't even there. Since the symbol isn't on the list, the search tool can't find it, no matter how we try to phrase the request.

For now, Full-Text Search is strictly for words and numbers, not symbols.
---
25/Feb/26 8:39 PM;712020:f09934f9-c0ad-4594-84e7-19f363dfd836;Understood, thank you again for explaining. I’ll get back to the client, letting them know about this [~accountid:5f7aab31bd1298006fe71331]. Please feel free to close the ticket accordingly 🙏

23/Feb/26 10:12 PM;5fb370400dd553006f291943;[~accountid:712020:03b03ff4-3ad4-47b2-bab3-614540900332] I had raised a request to twilio and this is the response. Please provide the relevant data so that they can investigate further.

Thank you for reaching out and letting us know about the issue with calls disconnecting immediately after being placed. I understand how disruptive this can be, and I’m here to help resolve it as quickly as possible.
  
 I have completed the investigation for the reported call that disconnects immediately after being placed (Call SID: CAe7f313f7fc9b8f6d85e42f5e8e79cd3f).
 Our SIP trace analysis shows that the call was successfully received by Twilio and routed to the downstream termination carrier. However, the terminating carrier returned a *SIP 603 Decline*, which is a final response indicating that the call was intentionally rejected by the destination network.
  
 Call Flow Summary:

* Twilio received the INVITE from the customer system and processed it normally.
* The call was handed off to the terminating carrier responsible for the destination.
* The terminating carrier responded with *603 Decline*, causing the call to disconnect immediately.
* Twilio relayed this response back, which is why the caller experiences an instant disconnect.



  
 A 603 response is generated by the far-end network and is commonly associated with:

* Destination-side call blocking or filtering
* Caller ID/CLI validation failure
* End-user or carrier-level rejection policies
* Spam protection or robocall mitigation on the terminating network



 Since the rejection originates outside of Twilio’s network, this is not a platform or routing failure on Twilio’s side.
  
 *Next Steps:*
 *We need to escalate this with our carrier partner to determine the exact reason for the rejection. To do so we need 3 or 4 recent*
 *Call examples of within 24 hours where you noticed this issue.*

We have explained it is not, and talked about the limitations on the parser and the extension.

They currently have over 2,000 candidates affected and are requesting that these records be updated with the missing information. We have advised them to export the data via CSV, update it manually, and re-import it into Recruit CRM. However, they have stated that this is not feasible for them and are requesting a solution that does not require any manual effort on their side.

They have asked for this issue to be escalated and are insisting on an automated or system-level fix or a fix done from our side.

I know this is a long shot, but given the circumstances and the client’s expectations, I wanted to check if there are any alternative solutions or possibilities we can explore from our side.

Summary: Last Communication Search Inconsistently Showing Sent Emails by Date
Description:
The client is experiencing *inconsistencies with the “Last Communication” search in Recruit CRM*. Although multiple emails sent on *20 January* are clearly visible in *Outlook*, they initially did not appear correctly in Recruit CRM when filtering by that date.

The root cause of the email delivery issue was identified as *bulk email limits*, which resulted in:

* Some emails being *successfully sent*
* Some emails landing in *Spam*
* Some emails *failing or not being sent at all*

This mixed outcome has created *inconsistencies in communication tracking*.

While a temporary workaround exists, it does *not solve the core problem*. The client is unable to *easily identify which contacts did or did not receive the email sent on 20 January*, especially given the large volume of recipients (*~310 contacts*).

Due to the inconsistent reflection of email activity in *Last Communication*, the client is currently *unable to reliably filter and isolate prospects* for whom the email was not sent, making follow-up and re-sending extremely difficult.

Screen recording of the issue:- [+https://app.screendesk.io/recordings/39c84da9-5d6e-4b4b-91e8-fe098e46a40b+|https://app.screendesk.io/recordings/39c84da9-5d6e-4b4b-91e8-fe098e46a40b]+.+

Summary: Importing contacts and companies
Description:
A client acc ID: 97546, wants to import their contacts, matching the companies to the contacts, and, according to the article, the character “&” can break the code, which is pretty common in company names.

A suggestion for the client was to change the company's name from “&” to “and”, but since the companies are matched by name, this would create duplicates.



Is there a way to avoid this? While importing contacts and companies, we can only select to merge duplicate contacts.

Please advise on how we can import the companies while also checking for duplicates.
Comments:
16/Feb/26 2:00 PM;712020:ce4d5d77-3fdb-4605-95dd-7dfb5fd16cba;Hi team, any update on this?
---
17/Feb/26 5:03 PM;712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065;Any updates on this?
---
18/Feb/26 6:02 PM;712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065;Any updates on this? Client is asking for updates as it’s been almost a week and he has another ticket now
---
18/Feb/26 9:31 PM;5f682f6d46a2ed0072de8aef;[~accountid:62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a] Please check
---
19/Feb/26 4:42 AM;62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a;[~accountid:712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065] There is no *“merge duplicate companies”* step during import — only contacts (and candidates) are merged.



We do have duplicate data settings. When creating or importing a company, the system checks for an existing company in the account using:

* Company name
* Website 
* LinkedIn

If a match is found, the row is linked to the existing company and no new company is created.

!image-20260218-230751.png|width=736,height=209,alt="image-20260218-230751.png"!


---
19/Feb/26 5:24 AM;712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065;I understand this [~accountid:62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a] , but in this case, the company name would be different in the CSV from the system.



For example, a company exists in the system called “A & B”, now a contact is linked to this company in the CSV, but to avoid issues while importing the CSV, they would change the company name to “A and B”, which is different from “A & B”, creating a duplicate since we won’t be able to merge it. And there will be cases where website and LinkedIn URL are not available as well.



It’s mentioned in the article that the “&” character can cause problems while importing:

!Screenshot 2026-02-18 at 20.50.11.png|width=656,height=87,alt="Screenshot 2026-02-18 at 20.50.11.png"!

So, is there a way that we can match these companies with the special character in the name, or another way to fix this after the import?



The client is waiting for this confirmation to import their contacts.
---
19/Feb/26 8:38 PM;62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a;[~accountid:712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065] Please ask them to import it by adding the records to a hotlist first so it doesn’t interfere with the existing data. If any issue occurs, we’ll be able to identify and resolve it quickly.
Alternatively, if possible, could we get the file? We can test it from our end to ensure the user doesn’t face any issues.
---
19/Feb/26 8:50 PM;5f682f6d46a2ed0072de8aef;[~accountid:712020:85569305-8bdb-42de-b343-67cd0955c065] This issue was resolved a few months ago; however, it appears the help article has not yet been updated. The “&” symbol is now fully supported, and there is no need to replace it.

Please ask the client to proceed with the import without modifying the company name in the sheet.

For reference, we tested the import using the company name “A & B,” and it was successfully processed.
---

Summary: Synsel Clarification Required: Parent–Child (Subsidiary) Company Relationship, API & Webhook Behaviour
Description:
Could you please explain how the *parent/child (subsidiary) relationship between companies* works in Recruit CRM, specifically:


# *Data Model & Stored Fields*

* Which fields or references are updated when:
** A company is linked as a *parent*?
** A company is linked as a *child/subsidiary*?
* Are any related records automatically re-linked, inherited, or shared between the parent and child companies (for example: contacts, deals, notes, jobs, etc.)?
* Is the relationship strictly *one parent → multiple children*, or can other structures exist (e.g., multi-level hierarchies, re-parenting, etc.)?

# *Webhooks / API Behaviour*

* Which webhooks are triggered when:
** A parent–child relationship is created?
** The relationship is updated (e.g., changing the parent)?
** The relationship is removed?
* What does the webhook payload look like in each of these scenarios? Specifically, which fields change or are included in the payload?
* Are there any differences between these events and a standard company update webhook that we should be aware of?

# *Documentation*

* If there is any existing internal or external documentation related to this feature, please share the relevant links.

*Goal:*
The client wants to ensure they replicate all relevant system behaviour accurately and avoid missing any side effects when users create or update parent–child company relationships in Recruit CRM.
Comments:
17/Feb/26 2:38 PM;712020:2f3c881a-d280-419d-a04a-c5cacac76b23;1.3 As per my current understanding this should be the answer for 1.3, the structure is strictly *one parent → multiple children*, and a child company under a parent cannot itself act as a parent company. Please confirm if this is correct or if multi-level hierarchies are supported.
---
17/Feb/26 3:49 PM;712020:2f3c881a-d280-419d-a04a-c5cacac76b23;Hi [~accountid:63e07ac3491b20ef64bb1c0b] 

Kanchan said that you can check these. Thank you.

17/Feb/26 7:47 PM;5f682f6d46a2ed0072de8aef;[~accountid:62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a] Please check this.
---
17/Feb/26 8:15 PM;62a6cac4979e6e006903e76a;h2. 1. The 3 Endpoint calls (what each does) 

h4. {color:#bf2600}[It’s an Albatross API, not a Public API. The Public API does not support link/unlink company functionality.{color}

||#||Endpoint||Body||What it does||
|*1*|{{POST /v1/companies/unlink-child}}|{{{"child_company_slug":"16956621527700488929QTd"}}}|Removes the parent–child link for that company. That company becomes standalone ({{parent_company_id}} = null). If the old parent has no other children, its {{haschildren}} is set to 0.|
|*2*|{{POST /v1/companies/link-to-parent-company}}|{{parent: 16956621527700488929QTd}}, {{children: ["16938313882350488929HaN"]}}|Makes *16956621527700488929QTd* the parent and *16938313882350488929HaN* its child. So: A = parent, B = child.|
|*3*|{{POST /v1/companies/link-to-parent-company}}|{{parent: 16938313882350488929HaN}}, {{children: ["16956621527700488929QTd"]}}|Reverses the relationship: *16938313882350488929HaN* becomes parent and *16956621527700488929QTd* becomes its child. So: B = parent, A = child.|

So: *unlink* removes the relationship for one company; *link-to-parent-company* always sets one company as parent and one or more others as children. The “direction” is only determined by which slug you send as {{parent_company_slug}} and which as {{child_company_slugs}}.

h2. 2. Data model (what’s stored)

* *Child company* row: {{parent_company_id}}, {{parent_company_slug}}, {{hasparent = 1}}.
* *Parent company* row: {{haschildren = 1}} (when it has at least one child).
* *On* *unlink*: child’s parent fields are cleared; parent’s {{haschildren}} is set to 0 if it has no other children.

Only these company fields change; no other tables (contacts, deals, jobs, notes) are updated automatically.

h2. 3. Related records (contacts, deals, jobs, notes)

* *Nothing is re-linked or moved.* Contacts, deals, jobs, and notes stay attached to the same company they were attached to.
* Parent/child is used *only for grouping in the app*: e.g. “show contacts/deals for this company and its parent/children.” So you see a combined view; the underlying links (e.g. contact → company) do not change.

h2. 4. Structure (hierarchy and re-parenting)

* *One level only:* one parent ↔ many children. No grandparent/parent/child chains.
* *One parent per company:* each company has at most one {{parent_company_id}}.
* *Re-parenting:* Unlink the child, then call link-to-parent-company with the new parent.

h2. . Webhooks / API behaviour

* *Link* and *unlink* do *not* trigger any webhooks.

* So there is *no webhook payload.*


---

Summary: HMP - Login issues for one of the hiring managers
Description:
Hi team,

Bre from *Therapy Partners Group* reached out, reporting an issue with the *Hiring Manager Portal (HMP)*. One of their hiring managers, who has had access to the portal for over a year, is now being prompted to *request access* again when trying to sign in.

Here’s the context shared by Bre:

* The hiring manager is logging in using [*mlopes@tpgpt.com*|mailto:mlopes@tpgpt.com]
* He has been actively using the Hiring Portal for more than a year, and recently, the system started asking him to request access again
* This setup was previously handled by Rutvik
* The person currently listed as the *primary hiring manager* (the first user who set up the portal) should no longer hold that role
* Due to their company structure (multiple brand partners), Bre needs either:
** Access to approve users herself, or
** A setup where users are not required to request approval each time

Could you please help investigate the following:

# Who is/was the *primary email ID* from the *@tpgpt.com* domain that initially set up the Hiring Manager Portal?
# What may have caused an existing user to suddenly be asked to request access again?
# Whether it’s possible to *transfer the primary access* to another user (ideally Bre), or adjust the setup so approvals are no longer required?

Let me know if you need any additional details from my side.

CC: [~accountid:61138e33fc68c100694b9891] [~accountid:712020:9e4be7f2-ce08-4d42-a7e8-6c5789fc853d]
Comments:
23/Jan/26 4:15 PM;633ad9c62eaaa5dcfa163a78;[~accountid:712020:2635ce73-39e4-4a85-a676-9841dded5d5a] Once you find anything , please update here 
---
27/Jan/26 11:45 AM;712020:2635ce73-39e4-4a85-a676-9841dded5d5a;Hi [~accountid:712020:f09934f9-c0ad-4594-84e7-19f363dfd836] below are the findings


# *Primary email ID*
The primary email ID that was initially used to set up the Hiring Manager Portal is [*sreynolds@tpgpt.com*|mailto:sreynolds@tpgpt.com].

# *Reason an existing user is being asked to request access again*
Based on the records, there is no existing user account associated with the email [*mlopes@tpgpt.com*|mailto:mlopes@tpgpt.com]. Since this email has not previously signed up on the platform, the system is treating it as a new user, which is why it is prompting for a fresh access request.

# *Transferring primary access / removing approval requirement*
Yes, this is possible, but it cannot be done from the user’s side. Transferring the primary access to another user (for example, Bre) or modifying the setup to remove approval requirements requires a backend change that must be handled by the engineering team.

19/Jan/26 2:09 PM;712020:ad34511a-defc-4eae-8324-da3b407f45cc;hi [~accountid:6119ebfe46c6b50071679c96] 

We investigated the issue where the image was not loading in the scheduler, even though the image URL was accessible directly in the browser.

After checking with the Nylas team, the root cause was identified as follows:

* The original image URL (hosted on PitchBook) was returning a *403 Forbidden* error when accessed by *Nylas servers*.
* This means the image host was blocking requests from Nylas (likely due to access restrictions or server-side rules), even though it appeared valid in normal browser access.
* As a result, Nylas was unable to fetch and display the image in the scheduler.

*Resolution:*

* We uploaded the image to our *AWS S3 bucket* with public access.
* New image URL:
{noformat}https://production-recruitcrm-mumbai.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/662/sendemail/66359696dea6559a5139550833088145{noformat}
* you can use this above url for upload

*Key takeaway / Recommendation:*

* Images used in the Nylas Scheduler must be:
** Publicly accessible
** Served over HTTPS
** Not restricted by authentication, IP blocking, or anti-bot rules
* Hosting images on S3, a CDN, or the company website is the recommended approach.

19/Jan/26 7:27 PM;60a203dbb050a70069053d00;[~accountid:5f682f6d46a2ed0072de8aef] I checked this in multiple ways and found one issue related to an asynchronous request call. Currently, this request is being sent to the backend directly from the frontend. I verified that the requests are reaching the backend, but the issue on our side is that we are not showing a processing state and are closing the confirmation popup immediately. Because of this, the user can trigger multiple delete requests, which increases the chance of failures. I also checked the audit logs and Elasticsearch logs, but we only retain records for the last 10 days.

What I plan to do is implement this as a backend process within a single delete {{users/deactivate/}} endpoint, similar to what I am planning to do in plan page. This should resolve this issue.

Summary: Multi-select field values not replacing existing values on update
Description:
The user reports that when updating a *multi-select field*, the new value is *added to the existing values instead of replacing them*. This behavior persists even after a hard refresh.

!image-20260120-151058.png|width=966,height=281,alt="image-20260120-151058.png"!

In bulk update scenarios, the system provides options to *add* or *replace* existing values, and selecting *Replace* should overwrite the old values. If this option is not used, values continue to accumulate.Even the values which is showing up is actually been deleted, as shown in the screenshot below

!image-20260120-151041.png|width=577,height=254,alt="image-20260120-151041.png"!

If you add any option, it will be added to the existing options. If you want to remove an option from the multi-select, you need to click on the “–” icon next to the option.

!image-20260120-184253.png|width=1020,height=506,alt="image-20260120-184253.png"!

Summary: Quick USA | Deal split amount is slightly incorrect potentially due to round down
Description:
Recording of issue from client: [https://app.screendesk.io/recordings/3b6fd567-e0d3-4a18-bf93-c51206ec4f6c|https://app.screendesk.io/recordings/3b6fd567-e0d3-4a18-bf93-c51206ec4f6c]

User ID: 104999, Kana Maruyama is splitting 7.5% of the deal amount, which is $1078.35 but their accounting team would like to know why it's $1078.34 on RCRM and if this is fixable.

Deal slug: 17655782456530039943GRY

!video-7ucWQQ78Ke4p20260120-67-r5905v.mp4|width=1920,height=792,alt="video-7ucWQQ78Ke4p20260120-67-r5905v.mp4"!
Comments:
23/Jan/26 10:00 AM;5f682f6d46a2ed0072de8aef;[~accountid:712020:383767eb-1ddb-4ad0-bd68-a348acf3e6d9] [~accountid:622f37093eacc50071fc5e7b] Please check

23/Jan/26 10:13 AM;622f37093eacc50071fc5e7b;checking
---
23/Jan/26 12:23 PM;622f37093eacc50071fc5e7b;[~accountid:712020:921da2d9-2e93-4832-b9ce-1e2fe2ce74b4] 

This is *expected behavior*, not a bug. We intentionally use *floor rounding* (rounding down) instead of standard rounding for deal split percentages.

h3. Why We Use Floor Rounding

When splitting deals equally among multiple parties, standard rounding can cause the *total to exceed 100%*.

*Example with 6 teams (equal split):*

* {{100 ÷ 6 = 16.666666...%}}

* With standard rounding → {{16.67%}}

* {{16.67% × 6 = 100.02%}} ❌ *Exceeds 100%*

This would mean we're attributing *more than the actual deal value*, which causes accounting discrepancies and is incorrect.

*With floor rounding:*

* {{100 ÷ 6 = 16.666666...%}}

* With floor → {{16.66%}}

* {{16.66% × 6 = 99.96%}} ✅ *Stays under 100%*

h3. Why This Affects the Reported Case

The $0.01 difference is a result of the same floor rounding logic being applied to ensure split amounts never exceed the total deal value. The system rounds *down* to the nearest cent to prevent over-allocation.

h3. Resolution

This is *working as designed*. The slight under-allocation (fractions of a cent) is preferable to over-allocating deal values, which would create accounting inconsistencies.

Summary: Target reports do not align with Sunday–Thursday workweek
Description:
A client based in Israel follows a Sunday–Thursday workweek. Since the system workweek cannot be customized, they use date ranges to review reports.

The issue is with target reports. When the report is set to weekly, the data gets split into two bars:

* One bar for the previous system week (because Sunday is treated as part of the previous week)
* One bar for the current week

The client wants to see one consolidated bar / single value that reflects the selected date range (their actual workweek), rather than having the target split across two weeks.

As a workaround, they switched the report to daily, but this causes another issue: the target is weekly, so the daily view makes it look like the target is never achieved and requires manual calculation.

Is there a way to align weekly target reports with a custom workweek (Sunday–Thursday), or to display a single consolidated value for a selected date range instead of splitting it across system-defined weeks?

Summary: Boolean search - Accented characters
Description:
If I run a Boolean search with a keyword containing an accented character, for example *“Maurício”*, only records where this exact word *with the accented character* is present are returned. Records where the keyword appears as *“Mauricio”* (without the accent) are not included.

Similarly, when I run a Boolean search using the keyword *“Mauricio”*, it does not return records where the name is stored as *“Maurício”*.

However, when I apply a filter on the *Name* column and use *Name contains Maurício*, it returns records that contain *“Mauricio”* (without the accent) as well. Likewise, using *Name contains Mauricio* also returns records where the name is stored as *“Maurício”*.

*Query:*
Why does Boolean search treat accented and non-accented characters as different values (accent-sensitive), while the filter search treats them as equivalent (accent-insensitive)? Is this difference intentional, and can Boolean search be made accent-insensitive to ensure consistent behavior across boolean search and filters?
Comments:
12/Jan/26 2:38 PM;712020:9e4be7f2-ce08-4d42-a7e8-6c5789fc853d;!Screen Recording 2026-01-12 at 2.20.59 PM.mov|thumbnail!
---
12/Jan/26 2:51 PM;5f7aab31bd1298006fe71331;[~accountid:712020:9e4be7f2-ce08-4d42-a7e8-6c5789fc853d] 


* Boolean search uses full-text search under the hood, which is accent-sensitive by default. It differentiates between characters like ç vs c, í vs i, etc. 
* Filters use substring matching (LIKE) with accent-insensitive collations, so Maurício ≈ Mauricio in that context.
